title: artifact-ui — How This Tool Works
description: artifact-ui is an MCP tool that turns a JSON 'system_spec' into an interactive, multi-view HTML diagram (Architecture, Layers, Sequences, Code Detail, Matrix, Components). This page documents the tool's own architecture at the system level (below) and, via the Code Detail tab, drills into both the key rendering/persistence functions in server.py and system_spec.py, and the system_spec JSON format itself — i.e. what an agent actually generates.

nodes.label: AI Agent
nodes.description: Claude (or another MCP client) calling the artifact-ui tools to explain code, plans, or architecture.

nodes.label: MCP Server
nodes.description: stdio JSON-RPC MCP server + a FastAPI app on :8765, sharing one asyncio event loop. Exposes get_example, render_artifact, save_artifact, get_artifact_spec.

nodes.label: system_spec
nodes.description: Core renderer: validates specs, lays out the architecture diagram (Kahn's longest-path layout), and renders all six views.

nodes.label: system_spec_examples
nodes.description: Library of example specs (sys_microservices, sys_event_driven, sys_monolith, code_impl_plan, code_bug_fix, mixed_levels) returned by get_example.

nodes.label: template_loader
nodes.description: Builds the render_artifact tool description (use cases, schema, vocab) shown to the agent.

nodes.label: design_system
nodes.description: Shared HTML page shell, CSS tokens, and the page_wrapper() used by every rendered artifact.

nodes.label: system_spec Format
nodes.description: The JSON schema an agent writes as the 'data' argument to render_artifact: nodes, edges, groups (optionally with a nested 'detail' for the Code Detail tab), and sequences.

nodes.label: Artifact Storage
nodes.description: Ephemeral previews under /tmp/artifact_ui/{id}/{id}.html + {id}.spec.json, plus optional persistent paths (e.g. this index.html + index.spec.json) written when 'path' is given to render_artifact.

nodes.label: Browser Tab
nodes.description: Opened via webbrowser.open() to preview the rendered artifact, with an injected Save button for ephemeral artifacts.

nodes.label: render_artifact handler
nodes.description: The render_artifact branch of call_tool(). Writes the spec alongside the HTML for the ephemeral /tmp artifact, and additionally writes both to an optional persistent 'path' (no Save-button JS injected there).
nodes.signature: async def call_tool(name, arguments) -> list[TextContent]  # 'render_artifact' branch
nodes.code_snippet:
rendered_html = renderer(data)
html = inject_runtime(rendered_html, artifact_id)
artifact_dir = ARTIFACT_DIR / artifact_id
artifact_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(artifact_dir / f"{artifact_id}.html").write_text(html, encoding="utf-8")
(artifact_dir / f"{artifact_id}.spec.json").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

persist_path = arguments.get("path")
if persist_path:
    dest = Path(persist_path)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    dest.write_text(rendered_html, encoding="utf-8")
    spec_path_for(dest).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

nodes.label: spec_path_for()
nodes.description: Maps an artifact's .html path to its sibling spec path, e.g. index.html -> index.spec.json.

nodes.label: save_artifact()
nodes.description: MCP tool. Promotes an ephemeral /tmp artifact (html + spec.json) to a persistent path, e.g. into this repo, creating directories as needed.

nodes.label: get_artifact_spec()
nodes.description: MCP tool. Reads back the sibling '<name>.spec.json' for a persistent artifact's .html path, so an agent can edit and re-render it in place.

nodes.label: serve_artifact()
nodes.description: FastAPI route serving the /tmp preview, reading from the per-artifact directory layout.

nodes.label: parse_spec()
nodes.description: Validates the spec via _validate_nodes_edges(), reused for the top-level spec AND for each group's optional 'detail' block (its own id namespace).

nodes.label: layout_graph()
nodes.description: Assigns node positions using Kahn's longest-path layering, co-locating nodes from the same group within a layer.

nodes.label: render_architecture_svg()
nodes.description: Renders the architecture diagram SVG. Takes an id_prefix param so multiple diagrams (one per Code Detail group) can coexist on one page without id collisions, and applies change-status coloring when a node has status added/modified/deleted.

nodes.label: render_detail_panels()
nodes.description: Renders the per-node sidebar detail panels (description, signature, code snippet). Takes the same id_prefix param to match prefixed node ids.

nodes.label: render_code_detail_html()
nodes.description: For each group with a 'detail' block, renders a nested mini architecture diagram + detail panels (using id_prefix), selectable via a 'Module' dropdown — this is the Code Detail tab you're reading right now.
nodes.code_snippet:
def render_code_detail_html(spec: dict) -> str:
    detail_groups = [g for g in spec["groups"] if g.get("detail", {}).get("nodes")]
    if not detail_groups:
        return ""
    ...
    for g in detail_groups:
        sub = parse_spec({"title": g["label"], "nodes": g["detail"]["nodes"],
                          "edges": g["detail"].get("edges", [])})
        positions = layout_graph(sub["nodes"], sub["edges"], [])
        prefix = f'cd-{g["id"]}-'
        svg    = render_architecture_svg(sub, positions, id_prefix=prefix)
        panels = render_detail_panels(sub, id_prefix=prefix)
        ...

nodes.label: render_changes_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Changes tab from a flat node list. render_system_spec passes top-level nodes PLUS every node from every group's 'detail', so a modified function inside a Code Detail group shows up there too. Only invoked when at least one node has status added/modified/deleted — not the case for this artifact.

nodes.label: render_system_spec()
nodes.description: Top-level entry point. Builds all_nodes (top-level + all group.detail nodes), computes render_code_detail_html(), and inserts a 'Code Detail' tab between Sequences and Matrix when any group has detail (a 'Changes' tab is inserted instead/also when any node has a change-tracking status).

nodes.label: Node
nodes.description: A box in the Architecture diagram. 'kind' picks its icon/color (service, db, queue, external, module, function, class, file, package). Code-level fields (signature, code_snippet, previous_code_snippet, file_path, line_range, status) add the detail-panel snippet, location metadata, and Changes-tab entries.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "id": "orders",              // unique, referenced by edges/groups/sequences
  "label": "Order Service",
  "kind": "service",           // service|db|queue|external|module|function|class|file|package
  "description": "...",        // shown in the detail panel
  "tech": "Go",                // sub-label
  "owner": "team-name",
  "status": "stable",          // stable|experimental|deprecated|planned
                               // or added|modified|deleted -> Changes tab + colored border
  "tags": ["critical"],
  "signature": "func ...",      // code-level: shown above the snippet
  "code_snippet": "...",        // code-level: syntax-highlighted
  "previous_code_snippet": "...", // code-level: enables before/after diff
  "file_path": "src/...",
  "line_range": [10, 42]
}

nodes.label: Edge
nodes.description: An arrow from one node to another. 'kind' sets its color/style (calls, imports, depends, emits, subscribes, reads, writes, deploys, owns, returns, throws, overrides, implements, instantiates). 'async: true' or certain kinds render as dashed.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "from": "ui",                // node id
  "to": "api",                  // node id
  "kind": "calls",
  "label": "REST",              // optional, shown on the arrow
  "async": false,               // optional -> dashed line
  "protocol": "HTTP"            // optional, informational
}

nodes.label: Group
nodes.description: A labeled bounding box around a set of node ids. kind='layer' groups also become swim-lanes in the Layers tab. Other kinds (package, team, domain, deployment) are drawn but don't add a Layers lane.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "id": "frontend",
  "label": "Frontend",
  "kind": "layer",              // layer|package|team|domain|deployment
  "members": ["ui", "client"],  // node ids
  "detail": { ... }             // optional, see Group.detail
}

nodes.label: Group.detail
nodes.description: An optional nested spec on a group: its own 'nodes' and 'edges' (own id namespace, typically function/class/file kinds). Each group with a non-empty detail.nodes gets an entry in the Code Detail tab's module dropdown, rendered as its own mini architecture diagram + detail panels. This Spec Language section is itself a Group.detail.

nodes.label: Sequence
nodes.description: An ordered list of steps (from -> to, with a label) between top-level node ids, rendered as a sequence/swimlane diagram in the Sequences tab with a dropdown to pick between multiple sequences.

sequences.label: Iterate on a persistent artifact later
